html:
Exclusive to:

Swordfish are hostile Mobs found in the Shipwrecked DLC. They spawn in the Deep Ocean from an invisible spawner. One will spawn in the same spot every 60–120 seconds, as long as the player is nearby.

Despite being hostile, they are considered innocent creatures and killing one adds 4 points to the Player's naughtiness level, which can cause Krampus to appear.

Dead Swordfish

When a Swordfish is killed it becomes a Dead Swordfish. It can be eaten to restore 1 and 25 . If cooked at a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea, or Dwarf Star, it will produce Fish Steak. Dead Swordfish can be used to craft the Cutlass Supreme. It can also be dried to get Jerky, or be traded to Yaarctopus for Dubloons. A Dead Swordfish cannot be used to cook in the Crock Pot.

When a Dead Swordfish is left to spoil, it will turn into a Spoiled Fish.

Fighting Strategy

To fight Swordfish, boat speed of 7 or above is recommended, adding both Boat and the Sail speed. Sailing close to the Swordfish will cause it to attack, which can be dodged by sailing in the opposite direction after the Swordfish pauses before the attack. Then players will be able to land 1 to 2 hits with a melee weapon depending on boat speed. Swordfish cannot be stunlocked.

The player can also dock near a herd of Water Beefalo and let the Swordfish attack the Beefalo. The Beefalo, fighting in groups, will usually win, resulting in the Swordfish's death. However, the player needs to actually get on land for this strategy to work, as the Swordfish will not attack other mobs while chasing the player.

If playing as Wendy, one can let Abigail attack the Swordfish and even persist until it dies, if given enough time. Note that the Swordfish doesn't change target when fighting, so the player must be wary and ensure to continuously fleeing while Abigail finishes the job. This, on the other hand, can make the process of killing a Swordfish completely safe.

Usage

×2

Tips

  • Once the location of the spawner is found, it can be marked by Items that have Map markers, like any one of the Backpacks, Traps, Sea Traps, or Boats as well as a Buoy.
  • It'd be wise to save the Swordfish for crafting the Cutlass Supreme as the weapon can serve as a sturdier and saner alternative to the Dark Sword.

Trivia

  • There is code in the game files that would allow using alive Swordfish in the Crock Pot, counting as 1 unit of Fish and 0.5 units of Meat. However, there is no method implemented that would make capturing Swordfish alive possible.

Gallery

  • A Swordfish.
  • A frozen Swordfish.
  • A Dead Swordfish on the ground beside Wendy.
  • Swordfish drawn during the Klei Doodle Jam.
  • Swordfish from game files.

"swordfish"
“I think this fish evolved to run me through.”
–Wilson, when examining a Swordfish.
“En garde!”
–Willow, when examining a Swordfish.
“Wolfgang challenges you to duel!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Swordfish.
“This fish cuts death with its face!”
–Wendy, when examining a Swordfish.
“FIGHTING FISH”
–WX-78, when examining a Swordfish.
“Don't upset the broadbill!”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Swordfish.
“That there's one pointy fish.”
–Woodie, when examining a Swordfish.
“Ah, a sparring partner!”
–Maxwell, when examining a Swordfish.
“What a magnificently cuspidated creature!”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Swordfish.
“Nöw yöu are a fish after my heart!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Swordfish.
“We like your sword face, fish!”
–Webber, when examining a Swordfish.
“I've resolved to be more of a lover than a fighter.”
–Walani, when examining a Swordfish.
“A deluxe, but dangerous ingredient!”
–Warly, when examining a Swordfish.
“Arrr ye'd like ta bury thet nose in ol'Woodlegs eh?”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Swordfish.
“PUT UP THY SWORD”
–Wilba, when examining a Swordfish.
“Sharpnose Glub Glub”
–Wormwood, when examining a Swordfish.
“Swell. A swimming swashbuckler.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Swordfish.

“I better not run with this.”
–Wilson, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Ugh, fire would get rid of that smell.”
–Willow, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“It was a good fight.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Its sword face couldn't save it...”
–Wendy, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“LIFE IS NOT WORTH IT”
–WX-78, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Looks a tad less ferocious this way.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Sorry, guy.”
–Woodie, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Perhaps it should have taken more fencing lessons.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“What a perfectly pernicious proboscis!”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Önward tö Valhalla, brave fish.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Should we eat it or use it?”
–Webber, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Woah, that fish is HUGE!”
–Walani, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“The grand poisson!”
–Warly, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Ye put up a good fight.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“'TIS DEAD”
–Wilba, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“(sob)”
–Wormwood, when examining a Dead Swordfish.
“Your adventure is at an end. My adventure is just beginning."”
–Wheeler, when examining a Dead Swordfish.

It can be eaten to restore 1 {{Hp}} and 25 {{Hunger}}. If cooked at a [[Campfire]], [[Fire Pit]], [[Chiminea]], or [[Star Caller's Staff|Dwarf Star]], it will produce [[Fish Steak]]. Dead Swordfish can be used to craft the [[Cutlass Supreme]]. It can also be [[Drying Rack|dried]] to get [[Jerky]], or be traded to [[Yaarctopus]] for [[Dubloons]]. A Dead Swordfish cannot be used to cook in the [[Crock Pot]]. When a Dead Swordfish is left to spoil, it will turn into a [[Spoiled Fish]]. {{clear}} =={{Pic|32|Spear}} Fighting Strategy == To fight Swordfish, boat speed of 7 or above is recommended, adding both [[Boats|Boat]] and the [[Sail]] speed. Sailing close to the Swordfish will cause it to attack, which can be dodged by sailing in the opposite direction after the Swordfish pauses before the attack. Then players will be able to land 1 to 2 hits with a melee weapon depending on boat speed. Swordfish cannot be stunlocked. The player can also dock near a herd of [[Water Beefalo]] and let the Swordfish attack the Beefalo. The Beefalo, fighting in groups, will usually win, resulting in the Swordfish's death. However, the player needs to actually get on land for this strategy to work, as the Swordfish will not attack other mobs while chasing the player. If playing as [[Wendy]], one can let [[Abigail]] attack the Swordfish and even persist until it dies, if given enough time. Note that the Swordfish doesn't change target when fighting, so the player must be wary and ensure to continuously fleeing while Abigail finishes the job. This, on the other hand, can make the process of killing a Swordfish completely safe. =={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe |item = Dead Swordfish |result = Spoiled Fish}} {{Recipe |item = Dead Swordfish |tool = Cooking |result = Fish Steak}} {{Recipe |item = Dead Swordfish |tool = Drying Rack |result = Jerky}} {{Recipe |item = Dead Swordfish |tool = Birdcage |result = Egg}} {{Recipe |item1 = Dead Swordfish |count1 = 1 |item2 = Gold Nugget |count2 = 2 |item3 = Twigs |count3 = 1 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Cutlass Supreme}} {{Recipe |item = Dead Swordfish |tool = Yaarctopus Icon |result = Octopus Chest}} == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Once the location of the spawner is found, it can be marked by [[Item]]s that have [[Map]] markers, like any one of the [[Backpack]]s, [[Trap|Traps]], [[Sea Trap|Sea Traps]], or [[Boats]] as well as a [[Buoy]]. * It'd be wise to save the Swordfish for crafting the Cutlass Supreme as the weapon can serve as a sturdier and saner alternative to the [[Dark Sword]]. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * There is code in the game files that would allow using alive Swordfish in the Crock Pot, counting as 1 unit of [[Fishes|Fish]] and 0.5 units of [[Meats|Meat]].
